summary: DeVault provides a step-by-step tutorial for installing Arch Linux with full disk encryption, presented as a follow-up to his earlier post on taking control of personal privacy. He publishes the accompanying video directly on his blog rather than YouTube, citing his preference for open source, ad-free, and DRM-free distribution. The post outlines 22 steps covering disk partitioning, LUKS encryption setup, base system installation, and bootloader configuration. He solicits feedback on the video format and mentions plans for a similar Ubuntu tutorial.
key_points: Full disk encryption is presented as a practical privacy measure that ordinary users should adopt
DeVault deliberately avoids YouTube in favor of self-hosted video to maintain open source, ad-free, DRM-free principles
The tutorial walks through LUKS encryption with dm-crypt on Arch Linux in 22 concrete steps
He frames this as part of a series making privacy practices accessible rather than abstract
money_quotes: I'd prefer to use my blog instead of YouTube for publishing technical videos, since it's all open source, ad-free, and DRM-free.
After my blog post emphasizing the importance of taking control of your privacy, I've decided to make a few more posts going over detailed instructions on how to actually do so.
key_insight: Privacy advocacy is most effective when paired with concrete, accessible instructions that empower people to actually implement it.
